def: present continuous — the default picture tense
example: The woman is writing in a notebook.

def: state what exists in the photo
example: There are several boxes on the floor.
The man is wearing a yellow helmet.

def: describe what someone has in hand
example: She is holding an umbrella.
The students are looking at the screen.

def: passive for an action done to something
example: The car is being repaired in the garage.

sense: when the given word sets the structure (Q1–5)
def: give a reason; forces a two-clause sentence
example: He is running because he is late.

sense: when the given word sets the structure (Q1–5)
example: She is cooking while she talks on the phone.

sense: when the given word sets the structure (Q1–5)
example: Although it is raining, they are playing outside.

sense: when the given word sets the structure (Q1–5)
def: preposition of place; pairs with there is/are
example: There is a chair next to the desk.

sense: when the given word sets the structure (Q1–5)
example: A car is parked in front of the store.

sense: when the given word sets the structure (Q1–5)
def: preposition of time within an event
example: People are taking photos during the parade.

word: Thank you for your email regarding
example: Thank you for your email regarding the job opening.

word: I am writing in response to
example: I am writing in response to your advertisement.

word: I hope this email finds you well
example: I hope this email finds you well.
Thank you for reaching out about our services.
It was a pleasure to meet you at the conference.
Could you please send me the updated schedule?
I would like to know whether the item is in stock.
Would it be possible to reschedule our meeting?
I was wondering if you offer a discount for bulk orders.
Please let me know whether Friday works for you.

word: I would like to inform you that
example: I would like to inform you that your order has shipped.
Please note that the deadline is this Friday.
I would suggest booking the venue in advance.
Unfortunately, the product is currently out of stock.
As requested, I have attached the report.

word: Please let me know if you have any questions
example: Please let me know if you have any questions.

word: I look forward to hearing from you
example: I look forward to hearing from you soon.

word: Thank you for your time and consideration
example: Thank you for your time and consideration.
Best regards, Lan Nguyen.
Should you need anything further, please contact me.

def: introduce a common view to frame the topic
example: It is often argued that technology harms communication.
In today’s world, remote work is increasingly common.

word: In my opinion / I firmly believe that
example: In my opinion, the benefits clearly outweigh the drawbacks.
I support this view for two main reasons.
This essay will argue that flexible hours improve productivity.
First and foremost, remote work increases flexibility.
Moreover, it reduces commuting costs.

def: support a claim with a concrete case
example: For example, employees save hours each week.
As a result, productivity tends to rise.

def: concede the other side before countering
example: Although there are challenges, the benefits are greater.

def: explain the reasoning behind a point
example: This is because workers feel more in control of their time.
In conclusion, the advantages far outweigh the disadvantages.
To sum up, flexible work benefits both sides.

def: tie the conclusion to your arguments
example: For these reasons, I firmly believe it is a positive trend.
Overall, the shift toward remote work should be encouraged.
Therefore, I strongly believe companies should adopt it.
